title: Chat assistant vs agentic workflow
subtitle: Two ways to put an LLM to work on the same job: you drive turn by turn, or the model runs a loop with tools. Pick per task, not per team.

agent.text: Tools, permissions and prompts to wire first.

chat.text: You steer every turn and paste the context yourself.

agent.text: One brief, then review the result.

chat.text: You see every step before it happens.

agent.text: Review checkpoints and diffs after the fact.
agent.note: Permission modes decide how much runs unattended.

agent.text: Prompts, specs and scripts live in the repo.

chat.text: Drafting, thinking out loud, one-off questions.

agent.text: Multi-step jobs with a checkable result: refactors, reports, pipelines.

agent.note: Budget caps and smaller models for mechanical steps keep it in range.

caption: ++ / -- mark intensity along each axis (more / less), never good or bad. Most days use both: chat to shape the idea, an agent to carry it out.
